info.title: Attendance & Schedule API
info.description: OpenAPI specification for the Attendance & Schedule management API.

parameters.name: user_level_tier_id
parameters.description: Filter by user level tier ID

get.description: Returns the full weekly schedule for a department. One row per employee, 7 day columns.

get.summary: Get schedule for a single day
get.description: Returns assignments for all employees in a department on a specific date.

post.summary: Save (and optionally publish) a weekly schedule
post.description: Full-week replace for a department. Pass publish=true to publish, false to save as draft.

post.summary: Copy previous week's schedule as draft

get.description: Streams a CSV file of the weekly schedule for the specified department and week.
Downloads a pre-formatted .csv template for the specified week. Fill in shift names, off, sick, or leave in each cell then upload via /schedules/import-excel.

parameters.name: week_start
parameters.description: Monday date for the template week (Y-m-d). Defaults to the current week's Monday.

post.description: Imports a schedule from an .xlsx or .csv file and saves it as a draft.
Uploads and processes a fingerprint data file for a department and week.

get.description: Returns an attendance grid for a date range. One row per employee, one column per day. Supports week_start shorthand or explicit date_from/date_to range.

parameters.name: week_start
parameters.description: Start of the week (Y-m-d). When provided, automatically sets date range to 7 days. Takes priority over date_from/date_to.

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Start of custom date range (Y-m-d). Required if week_start is omitted.

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: End of custom date range (Y-m-d). Defaults to date_from if omitted.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by attendance status. Accepts a single value or comma-separated list.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by employee name or nickname

get.summary: Get attendance detail for a single employee-day cell

WeekStartQuery.name: week_start
WeekStartQuery.description: Monday date that starts the week (Y-m-d)

message.example: Previous week copied successfully as draft.
Schedule imported as draft. Review and publish when ready.

week_start: The week start field is required.

permissions.description: All effective permissions (union of all assigned roles). Only present when roles are loaded.

UserStats.description: Aggregate counts across all users (always global, unaffected by active filters)

by_role.description: Count of users per role name. 'no_role' is users with no role assigned.

by_department.description: User counts per department, sorted by count descending. id=null means unassigned.

roles.description: Optional list of role names to assign on creation.

AssignmentCell.description: A single day cell in the schedule grid for one employee

summary.description: Count of employees per assignment type for the day

AssignmentRow.description: One assignment row inside the save request

publish.description: Set true to publish the schedule

errors.description: Per-row errors that were skipped during save

reason.example: shift_id required when type is shift

publish_error.description: Present only when publish was requested but validation failed

week_start.description: The target week to copy INTO (must be a Monday)

AuditDay.description: Attendance data for one employee on one date
